purpose: Identifies the current stock-bond correlation regime and assesses persistence, with direct implications for portfolio construction, hedging, and term premium.
components: Four sustaining conditions for negative regime (demographics, globalization, disinflation, EM reserves) — all weakening
NK micro-foundation: supply-shock dominance → positive correlation
Factor evidence: value premium co-movement with correlation regime
Persistence statistics: positive regime >5 years has only 25-40% probability
current_assessment: Positive regime likely (~60% probability) with all four negative-regime conditions weakening. But persistence >5 years only 25-40% probable, tempering duration conviction but not directional.
limitations: Persistence probability estimates have wide confidence intervals
Conditioned on inflation-deflation resolution (~50/50) which caps overall confidence
Reflexive loop between correlation, hedging, and term premium complicates static analysis
Historical regimes involved different structural contexts than current
